title: ratelimit.rs: HashMap key allocates String on every acquire
description:
LOCATION: src/ratelimit.rs line 32

FINDING: try_acquire calls buckets.entry(key.to_string()) which allocates a new String on every call, including on the common cache-hit path. The entry API on HashMap<String, _> always takes an owned key. The idiomatic fix is to first check with buckets.get(key) and only allocate when inserting: if the bucket exists, update it without allocation. Alternatively, use the raw_entry unstable API or restructure as get + insert.

IMPACT: One String allocation per incoming HTTP request on the inbox hot path. Minor throughput cost, but avoidable with a two-step get/insert pattern.
close_reason: Documented: ponytail comment on entry API allocation with raw_entry upgrade path
